categories.name: Pipes & Tubes
categories.description: Premium quality seamless, welded, and ERW pipes and tubes for industrial applications.

categories.name: Elbows & Bends
categories.description: Directional change fittings including 45°, 90°, and 180° elbows and custom bends.

categories.name: Tees
categories.description: Equal and unequal tees for flow division in pipeline systems.

categories.name: Reducers
categories.description: Concentric and eccentric reducers for changing pipe sizes.

categories.name: Flanges
categories.description: High-pressure flanges in all classes and face finishes.

products.name: Seamless Pipes
products.seoDescription: High-pressure seamless pipes manufactured to ASTM, API, and DIN standards.
products.description: Extruded from solid billets without weld seams, designed for high-pressure and critical applications.

faq.question: What is the max size for seamless pipes?
faq.answer: We manufacture and supply seamless pipes up to 24 inches in outer diameter.

faq.question: Why use seamless over ERW?
faq.answer: Seamless pipes have no weld seam, making them structurally uniform and capable of withstanding about 20% higher pressure than welded pipes of the same material and size.

products.name: ERW Pipes
products.seoDescription: Electric Resistance Welded pipes for water, gas, and structural applications.
products.description: Electric Resistance Welded pipes manufactured from steel coils, offering excellent dimensional accuracy and cost-efficiency.

faq.question: What is ERW?
faq.answer: ERW stands for Electric Resistance Welded. The pipe is formed by rolling strip steel into a cylinder and welding the edges using a high-frequency electrical current.

products.name: Long Radius (LR) Elbows
products.seoDescription: Premium long radius elbows (R=1.5D) in carbon, alloy, and stainless steel.
products.description: Pipe fittings used to change the direction of flow by 90 or 45 degrees, where the centerline radius is 1.5 times the nominal pipe size.

faq.question: What does LR mean?
faq.answer: LR stands for Long Radius. It means the radius of curvature is 1.5 times the nominal pipe diameter (1.5D).

products.name: Weld Neck Flanges
products.seoTitle: Weld Neck Flanges ASME B16.5 | SRS
products.seoDescription: High-pressure weld neck flanges manufactured to precision tolerances.
products.description: Flanges designed to be butt-welded to the pipe, providing an excellent stress distribution through the tapered hub.

faq.question: Why choose Weld Neck over Slip-On?
faq.answer: Weld neck flanges transfer stress from the flange to the pipe, making them ideal for high-pressure and high-temperature applications where fatigue strength is critical.

materials.name: Carbon Steel
materials.corrosionResistance: Low; requires coating or painting in corrosive environments.
typicalApplications: Low to medium pressure gas lines

materials.name: Stainless Steel (Austenitic)
materials.corrosionResistance: High; excellent resistance to oxidation and many organic/inorganic chemicals. 316L adds resistance to chlorides.

materials.name: Chrome-Moly Alloy Steel
materials.corrosionResistance: Moderate; better oxidation resistance than carbon steel at high temperatures due to Chromium content.

materials.name: Duplex & Super Duplex
materials.corrosionResistance: Superior; exceptional resistance to chloride stress corrosion cracking, pitting, and crevice corrosion.

materials.name: Nickel Alloys & Exotics
materials.corrosionResistance: Extreme; resists severe corrosive environments including strong acids, alkalis, and seawater at elevated temperatures.

industries.name: Oil & Gas (Upstream & Downstream)
industries.description: Exploration, extraction, refining, and transportation of petroleum and natural gas.
industries.requirements: High pressure ratings, resistance to sour gas (H2S), strict traceability, and 100% NDT inspection.

industries.name: Power Generation
industries.description: Thermal, nuclear, and renewable power plants.
standards: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC)
industries.requirements: High-temperature creep resistance, high-pressure steam handling, and zero-defect welded joints.

industries.name: Chemical & Pharmaceutical
industries.description: Manufacturing of bulk chemicals, fine chemicals, and pharmaceuticals.
industries.requirements: Extreme corrosion resistance, sanitary surface finishes, and zero contamination risk.

industries.name: Water Treatment & Desalination
industries.description: Municipal water supply, wastewater treatment, and reverse osmosis desalination plants.
industries.requirements: Large diameter transmission, resistance to chloride pitting (for desalination), internal coatings (e.g., epoxy).
